$schema_note: Pass-2 harvest by MisconceptionAnalyst from common_miss in data/buckets/verb_form.imperativo.json. PENDING ratification. Uses families ratified in thread v2; new specifics defined here, reused ones under reuses_existing. Thread: inter_chat/Architecture_MisconceptionAnalyst_registry_harvest.md.

proposed_family_actions.note: Using a valid but wrong present-tense cell as the imperative.
English-style negation of a finite imperative.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: Wrong present-tense cell used as the imperative
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Using a valid present-indicative person as the tu imperative: the 2sg -i for the -are imperative (which is the 3sg -a), or the wrong vowel on -ere/-ire.
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: The -are imperative = 3sg present, a notorious cell confusion.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: Finite form negated instead of non + infinitive
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Negating the positive tu imperative directly (English 'don't speak') instead of the Italian non + infinitive.
proposed_misconceptions.cross_kind_note: transfer overlay (v2 B); the error mirrors English negating a finite verb.
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: Italian's tu negative imperative is suppletive (non + infinitive).

reuses_existing.note: va for va'/vai; building the irregular formal imperative from the infinitive.
sei! for sii! and the formal Lei imperative use the (subjunctive-based) form; using the indicative is the same mood-form error.
